channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alleged 'Zizians' leader was once declared dead in California
item.description: LaSota was still alive, however, when state police officers found and arrested her Sunday in Allegany County, Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Delphi murders: Gull rules against defense motions seeking to reexamine Richard Allen's conviction
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. -- Special Judge Fran Gull denied recent motions from Richard Allen’s attorneys seeking to reexamine his conviction last year in the Delphi murders case. In an order dated Feb. 14, Gull denied the defense’s Verified Motion to Correct Error (filed on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration gives schools a deadline to end DEI programs or risk losing federal money
item.description: The Trump administration is giving America’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate diversity initiatives or risk losing federal money, raising the stakes in the president’s fight against “wokeness” and sowing confusion as schools scramble to comply.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Far-left activist Leonard Peltier, serving life in murders of 2 FBI agents, leaves prison under Biden clemency
item.description: Killer and American Indian Movement activist Leonard Peltier to receive welcome home bash 49 years after conviction in FBI agents' murders at Pine Ridge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan mom who braved flames for son in hyperbaric chamber explosion stresses treatment dangers: attorney
item.description: The Michigan mom of 5-year-old Thomas Cooper, who died in a hyperbaric chamber on Jan. 31, jumped toward flames in the chamber to try to save her son after it exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netflix series brings new attention to Gabby Petito case
item.description: Gabby's family and friends gave interviews for the three-part Netflix series called "American Murder: Gabby Petito.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utahns participate in nationwide Presidents' Day protests against Trump, Musk
item.description: Hundreds of Utahns gathered at the State Capitol on Monday, Feb. 17, to participate in an hours-long protest against President Donald Trump — but not everyone at the Capitol shared the same viewpoint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KTLA 5

item.title: Top Democrats question IRS on DOGE access to taxpayer data
item.description: Top Democrats in Congress want to know what Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) plans to do at the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) after multiple outlets reported the advisory unit sought access to sensitive IRS systems. Senate Finance Committee members Ron Wyden (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shakira hospitalized, postpones concert
item.description: Shakira has been hospitalized just two days into her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour. The "Hips Don't Lie" singer said she suffered from an abominable condition that forced her to cancel her show in Lima, Peru. "I am very sad that I will not be able to take the stage today.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Stolen sea monster carousel figurine recovered 40 years later
item.description: "We have gotten a priceless piece of our history back," Quassy President Eric Anderson said in a written statement.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: Legendary Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio has passed away at 77 years old. Paquita, whose real name is Francisca Viveros Barradas, passed away at her home in Veracruz, Mexico. Her family revealed her passing in a post on Monday morning on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Kentucky mother, 7-year-old daughter among dozen dead from flooding
item.description: A mother and her 7-year-old daughter are among at least a dozen people dead in Kentucky after severe flooding struck the state, officials said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire at Washington state indoor gun range leaves 1 dead, 1 injured
item.description: The Tacoma Fire Department responded to a deadly fire at the Bull's Eye Indoor Range on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Cher, Lady Gaga, Miley Cyrus, Snoop Dogg: 'Saturday Night Live' is feted with a concert for the ages
item.description: By the time Cher sang “If I Could Turn Back Time,” it seemed as if time had indeed been turned back, and every single “Saturday Night Live” musical guest of 50 years had magically found their way to Radio City Music Hall. Of course, it was only a smattering.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday that her government wouldn't rule out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
